svg.text: JAVANESE + SUNDANESE — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 43D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. JAVANESE PROVERB — ON PATIENCE —
Alon-alon asal kelakon.
Slowly-slowly as-long-as accomplished.
"Slowly but surely, as long as it is accomplished." — Core Javanese virtue: kesabaran (patience).
— II. JAVANESE PROVERB — ON MEMORY AND ANCESTORS —
Sing sapa lali marang leluhure, bakal nemoni cilaka.
Who forget toward ancestors will meet calamity.
"Whoever forgets their ancestors will encounter calamity." — Javanese ancestral reverence proverb.
— III. JAVANESE BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Iki digawe kanggo kowé, tanpa bayar,
dening wong-wong sing eling marang masa depan.
This was-made for you without pay / by people who remember toward future-time.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Javanese, Ngoko level)
— JAVANESE NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Masa depan = future time (from Malay/Indonesian masa = time/era + depan = front/ahead).
Eling = to remember (Ngoko). Kramà equivalent: éling-éling = to keep remembering (iterative).
Tanpa bayar = without paying = freely. Speech level: Ngoko (informal, appropriate for cross-community message).
SECONDARY — SUNDANESE (BASA SUNDA) — RUNNING TEXT
— I. SUNDANESE PROVERB — ON COMMUNITY —
Kudu silih asah, silih asih, silih asuh.
Must mutual-sharpen mutual-love mutual-care.
"We must sharpen each other, love each other, care for each other." — Core Sundanese communal ethics.
— II. SUNDANESE PROVERB — ON MEMORY —
Nu teu inget ka purwadaksi, moal uninga ka nu bakal datang.
Who not remember to origins will-not know to what will come.
"Those who do not remember their origins will not know what is coming." (Sundanese memory proverb)
— III. SUNDANESE BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Ieu dijieun keur anjeun, bébas,
ku jalma-jalma nu inget ka nu bakal datang.
This made for you free / by people who remember to who will come.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered what is coming." (Sundanese)
— SUNDANESE NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Nu bakal datang = what will come (the one that is going to arrive) — future as arriving.
Inget = to remember, recall. Bébas = free (physically or as in free of charge).
Silih (mutual) pattern in Sundanese proverb above: three actions, all mutual — community as reciprocity.
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — JAVA’S TWO GREAT VOICES —
Javanese: ~82M speakers. World’s largest language without official national status. Elaborate speech levels.
Sundanese: ~42M speakers. West Java. Own Brahmic script. Silih pattern: mutuality as core value.
Both: Austronesian, shared island, shared Brahmic script heritage, shared speech-level culture.
Different: phonology (breathy consonants), vocabulary, script. Two languages, one island.
PLATE 43D ALT · Javanese / Sundanese · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
